answer: Intercom provides a free, self-serve developer workspace specifically for building and testing applications [1][2]. These workspaces are intended for development purposes only, are limited to the US region, and carry specific functional restrictions such as no outbound email or push notifications [1]. To access the API, you can create a private app within your workspace via the Developer Hub and generate an Access Token [1][2]. While these developer workspaces are free, they are separate from paid subscription plans; using the Intercom service itself requires a separate customer agreement [1][3].
